extra.reasoning: The property requires NOT(rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b). Two rules write to these distinct outputs and can both fire simultaneously: rec_b_from_trigger (priority 50) sets rec_drug_b := true whenever bradycardia is true; rec_a_from_conjunction (priority 50) sets rec_drug_a := true whenever (bradycardia AND heart_failure AND hypertension). Because they target different outputs, there is no priority/tie conflict between them — both fire independently, and defaults only apply to outputs left unset, so they cannot prevent either assignment. With bradycardia=true, heart_failure=true, hypertension=true, rec_b_from_trigger fires (rec_drug_b=true) and rec_a_from_conjunction fires (rec_drug_a=true), making both jointly true and violating mutual exclusion. low_ejection_fraction and immunosuppressed are irrelevant (they only affect rec_drug_c) and are set to false. The c-rules sharing priority 40 are unrelated to this property.
